prose:
Method provenance
Property: HOMO–LUMO (frontier-orbital) energy gap, eV
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted RI) in PySCF
Geometry provenance: single-point on user-supplied geometry in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz (no geometry optimization; DFT//input-XYZ)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvation)
Charge / multiplicity: 0 / 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software: PySCF (version as bundled in chemkit backend for this run)
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 8 cycles; no warnings reported by the engine; no fallbacks fired. Density fitting (RI) approximation active by default in PySCF.

Result
HOMO energy = −7.6523067 eV
LUMO energy = −1.7826355 eV
HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.8696712 eV

Reproducibility
• Command executed (from result `cli_invocation`):
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --multiplicity 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpboo0kemk/live.json
• Result JSON: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpboo0kemk/live.json
• Live engine output was written during the run to a .out log in the same directory (path available on request).

No warnings were reported during the calculation.

Therefore, the computed gas-phase B3LYP/def2-TZVP (RI) HOMO–LUMO gap of formaldehyde at the supplied geometry is 5.8696712 eV.
